name: outlook-calendar-automation
description: Automate Outlook Calendar tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): create events, manage attendees, find meeting times, and handle invitations. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Create new calendar events with subject, time, location, and attendees
List, search, and filter calendar events by date range or keywords
Update existing events including time, location, and attendee changes
Delete events or decline meeting invitations
Find available meeting times across multiple attendees
Generate Microsoft Teams meeting links for events
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' patterns are markdown code formatting (backtick-enclosed tool names like `RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS`), not actual shell execution. The 'network' finding is a legitimate MCP server URL required for skill configuration. No cryptographic code or malicious patterns present. This is a standard documentation file for an Outlook Calendar automation skill.
valueStatement: Schedule meetings, manage attendees, and find optimal meeting times directly from Claude without manually opening Outlook. This skill streamlines calendar operations through a simple MCP integration.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP connection with Outlook toolkit configured
Only works with Microsoft Outlook calendars (not Google Calendar)
Cannot access calendars without proper OAuth authentication
Event search is limited to OData filter-supported fields only

useCases.targetUser: Team leads, project managers, and employees who schedule frequent meetings
useCases.title: Schedule Team Meetings
useCases.description: Quickly create calendar events for team syncs, project reviews, or one-on-ones without switching to Outlook

useCases.targetUser: Meeting organizers coordinating across multiple team members
useCases.title: Find Meeting Availability
useCases.description: Automatically find optimal meeting times that work for all attendees based on their calendars

useCases.targetUser: Administrative assistants and anyone who frequently modifies meetings
useCases.title: Manage Calendar Events
useCases.description: Update meeting times, locations, or attendee lists and handle invitation responses

promptTemplates.title: Create Simple Event
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a calendar event for [meeting title] on [date] from [start time] to [end time] in [timezone]
promptTemplates.scenario: Schedule a basic meeting with required details

promptTemplates.title: Schedule Meeting with Attendees
promptTemplates.prompt: Schedule [meeting title] for [date] at [time] with [email1, email2] as attendees. Include [any additional details] in the description.
promptTemplates.scenario: Create an event and invite specific people

promptTemplates.title: Find Available Time Slot
promptTemplates.prompt: Find available meeting times for [number] attendees ([email list]) next week for a [duration] meeting
promptTemplates.scenario: Discover optimal meeting times across multiple calendars

promptTemplates.title: Update Existing Event
promptTemplates.prompt: Find my meeting titled '[event title]' and update the time to [new start] to [new end], or change the location to [new location]

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before making API calls
Verify connection status shows ACTIVE before running any calendar workflows
Use ISO 8601 datetime format with timezone for all time-related parameters
antiPatterns: Attempting to use the skill without configuring Rube MCP with Outlook toolkit
Using invalid timezone names (must be valid IANA or Windows timezone identifiers)
Forgetting that updating attendees replaces the entire list, not just adding new ones

faq.question: Do I need an Outlook account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Microsoft Outlook account. The skill uses OAuth to connect to your Outlook calendar through the Rube MCP service.

faq.question: Is this skill affiliated with Microsoft?
faq.answer: No, this is a community-built skill that uses Composio's Rube MCP to interact with Outlook. It is not an official Microsoft product.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Google Calendar?
faq.answer: No, this skill specifically targets Microsoft Outlook. For Google Calendar, you would need a different skill or MCP provider.

faq.question: Does this skill send emails to attendees?
faq.answer: Yes, when you add attendees to an event or modify existing attendees, Microsoft Outlook automatically sends invitation emails to those attendees.

faq.question: Can I create Teams meetings with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, set is_online_meeting to true and online_meeting_provider to 'teamsForBusiness' when creating an event to automatically generate a Teams meeting link.

faq.question: What happens if I delete a recurring event?
faq.answer: Deleting a recurring event master will delete all occurrences of that event. The skill does not currently support deleting single instances of recurring events.
